action.reasoning: Observation: the stable pure-torch feature baseline improved avg_precision from 0.0392 to 0.0781, with best_val_ap ~0.099 using 195 histogram/count features and a 2-layer MLP. Analysis: with only 3 iterations left, the highest-ROI move is to enrich fixed features further and train on more data, while keeping the same robust no-PyG pipeline. Likely gains come from continuous summary stats per categorical field (mean/std/min/max), graph density/reciprocity-style counts, and a wider hidden layer; training time of ~200s leaves room for one stronger run plus eval. Decision: update train.py/predict.py to add richer numeric summaries on node/edge categorical columns, increase train/val limits and epochs moderately, smoke-test locally, and submit eval on the new checkpoint.
action.command: python -m py_compile train.py predict.py && python train.py --epochs 10 --train_limit 65536 --val_limit 16384 --batch_size 512 --hidden 768 --drop 0.25 --lr 0.0015 --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --mmap && python -c "import os,shutil; os.makedirs('local_test',exist_ok=True); shutil.copy('/data/val.pt','local_test/test.pt')" && python predict.py --data_path local_test --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output local_pred.json
